{{Quote|Snowy-oh, snow-oh.|Icehead guards at the Snow Palace|<ref name="TSS" />}}
'''Iceheads''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with lean, [[Color/Legends|black]] bodies that held spiked, rectangular heads that resembled blocks of [[Water/Legends|ice]]. Their large mouths brimmed with jagged [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and their three-fingered hands sported sharp claws. While they could walk like other humanoid species, members of the species could travel faster by flipping onto their heads and sliding on icy surfaces.

A group of Iceheads lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. There, armed with specialized [[Icehead club|clubs]], they guarded the [[Snow Palace]] and went on missions for a sorcerer called the [[Snow King]] and his [[Gender/Legends|mistress]], [[Odra]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], they stole the [[season scepter]], an [[Force artifact/Legends|artifact]], from the [[Sun King]] on their masters' orders and plunged Endor into perpetual [[Winter/Legends|winter]]. A group of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] infiltrated the palace disguised as Iceheads, recovered the artifact, and restored the scepter and the season to their previous states.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|I just love how they do that. ''[laughs]''<nowiki />|Odra on Icehead locomotion|audio=OdraIceheads-Ewoks.flac|<ref name="TSS" />}}
The Iceheads were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Limb/Legends|bipedals]] who were comfortable in the cold. They had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] bodies with two arms that ended in three-clawed hands and two legs that thickened toward pointed feet.<ref name="TSS" />

[[File:IceheadsSkating-TSS.png|thumb|right|300px|Iceheads could travel quickly on slick surfaces by skating on their heads.]]
The head of members of the species resembled blocks of [[Water/Legends|ice]]. The head was colored white and shaped like a thin rectangular prism, wide in front and back, but thin on the sides. The Iceheads' [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were blue polygons that stretched from one side of the face to the other; the sides of their eyes could change angle to show emotion, appearing at times as quadrilaterals, and at others as triangles. The remainder of the head was given over to a large, shovel-like mouth with sharp and uneven edges. The lower jaw stuck out and overlapped the upper lip. The mouth, too, was flexible and capable of expressing emotion. A line ran through the center of the face and led to the top of the head, which tapered to several backward-curving spikes. With the exception of the eyes, the entire head was white and shiny, down to the inside of the mouth.<ref name="TSS" />

Iceheads had several adaptations to aid them in cold-weather conditions. They could operate in low temperatures with no problems and could run normally through even deep snow, since they did not sink into it. Their quickest form of locomotion, however, utilized their hard, ice-like heads: when on a slick surface such as ice, Iceheads could flip upside down and skate about on their heads. Once they reached their destination, they flipped back upright and began walking again.<ref name="TSS" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Stand back! I've got the season scepter! Or… an Icehead's club…|Teebo|<ref name="TSS" />}}
Several Iceheads lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] and served their leader,<ref name="TSS" /> a sorcerer<ref name="COE">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref> known as the [[Snow King]], and his [[Gender/Legends|mistress]], a [[Frost Sprite]] known as [[Odra]], the [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]] of Sleet. The Iceheads lived and worked in and around the [[Snow Palace]], and their duties included standing guard over the Snow King and Duchess of Sleet, three on each side of the rulers' thrones, and patrolling the grounds outside the palace single-file while chanting, "Snowy-O, snow-O!" Such Icehead guards wielded [[Icehead club|wooden clubs]] that had thin, round grips, but which curved and grew wide and flat toward the other end. Other Iceheads performed various errands, typically armed with only their natural claws and [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Endor's Iceheads could comprehend the language spoken by their superiors, although they themselves rarely spoke except to acknowledge orders.<ref name="TSS" />

==History==
{{Quote|Uh, the Iceheads just bagged it from me. Bummer! Okay, like, there I was, just hangin' out with some of the dudes ''[…]'' We're like sittin' there just diggin' the sunshine…like how great it was to be out there baking, y'know? When, like, all of a sudden, ZINGO! Instant deep freeze! 'Whoa, dudes! Oh no! Hey!' The Iceheads were like, gone. History. And it's been snowing ever since!|The Sun King on losing the Season Scepter|<ref name="TSS" />}}
[[File:IceheadsNabSeasonScepter-TSS.png|thumb|left|300px|A group of Iceheads stole the [[season scepter]] from the [[Sun King]] and plunged Endor into eternal winter.]]
By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="EwoksDate">{{EwoksDate|appearance of Iceheads|[[The Season Scepter]]}}</ref> several Iceheads had come under the command of the Snow King and his mistress, the Duchess of Sleet, on the Forest Moon of Endor. As Odra's influence grew stronger over the Snow King, she convinced him to steal a powerful [[Force artifact/Legends|artifact]] known as the season scepter from his brother, the Sun King. Four Iceheads were tasked with the deed, and they sneaked up on the Sun King while he was resting with his friends and grabbed the season scepter from the sand nearby. The weather on the Forest Moon instantly changed from summer-like heat to wintery snows. The Icehead troops skated away on their heads before the Sun King or his friends could apprehend them.<ref name="TSS" />

A short time later, Odra urged the Snow King to send a group of Iceheads to intercept four [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]—[[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]]—who were on their way from [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] with, she thought, a powerful item called the [[Sunstar/Legends|Sunstar]] in their possession. The Iceheads were to capture the quartet and bring them to the Snow Palace. The Iceheads ambushed the Ewoks, who moved into the area on a large, flying leaf. During the ensuing [[Skirmish near the Sun Palace|skirmish]], the Iceheads boarded the leaf and flew with the Ewoks through a dark tunnel. On the other side, they found themselves sharing the conveyance with a large [[snow monster]] instead. Nevertheless, two of the Snow King's minions managed to capture Kneesaa, and with the advantage of higher ground, the Iceheads caused an avalanche to throw off the other three Ewoks. With their white-furred prize in tow, the Iceheads returned to the Snow Palace and brought their prisoner to the throne room. Under the protection of their Icehead guards, the rulers demanded that Kneesaa hand over the Sunstar, but she refused.<ref name="TSS" />

[[File:SnowKingIceheadClub-TSS.png|thumb|right|300px|The Snow King presented an Icehead club to Wicket W. Warrick for his Belt of Honor.]]
Meanwhile, a group of club-wielding Iceheads was completing a patrol of the grounds when the three Ewoks from the previous encounter coated themselves in snow and surreptitiously joined the procession as it entered the palace. Back in the throne room, three strange figures appeared and claimed to be [[ice-on-a-stick]] vendors. The Snow King and Odra, apparently seeing through the disguise, ordered their Icehead guards to attack. The servants dutifully flipped onto their heads and slid toward the Ewok Teebo, but he used one of their own clubs to knock them away. In the ensuing [[Skirmish at the Snow Palace|fray]], Teebo gained possession of the season scepter and began a futile effort to use it. Wicket W. Warrick joined his companion, and together, the two hit the Snow King with a [[Magic/Legends|magical]] blast that broke the thrall of the Duchess of Sleet and restored his appearance and personality to normal. Another blast shrank the Frost Sprite and melted her into a puddle of water.<ref name="TSS" />

In the battle's aftermath, the wintery weather subsided. Several other Ewoks, [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] and Chief [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], arrived at the palace in the company of the Sun King. The Snow King handed over the stolen scepter, and Endor returned to summer. In appreciation for his help, the Snow King gave Wicket W. Warrick an Icehead club for his [[Belt of Honor]].<ref name="TSS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Iceheads appear in "[[The Season Scepter]]," an episode of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series that first aired during the show's [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] in [[1986]].<ref name="TSS" /> Animation cells show that the characters were known as "Iceheads" from the production phase. The Iceheads were animated using two cells per pose: one for their eyes, and one for the rest of their bodies.<ref name="Ewoks TV series">[[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animation cell.</ref>
